title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE PIS-ANAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL ГўВЂВ” SIBALOM ANNEX IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF SIBALOM, PROVINCE OF ANTIQUE FROM THE PIS-ANAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS SIBALOM NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. No. 5176; 106 OG No. 13, 1694 (March 29, 2010)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 9964, January 15, 2010 ]

AN ACT SEPARATING THE PIS-ANAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL ГўВЂВ” SIBALOM ANNEX IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF SIBALOM, PROVINCE OF ANTIQUE FROM THE PIS-ANAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS SIBALOM NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representative of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Separation and Conversion into a National High School.

ГўВЂВ” The Pis-anan National High School ГўВЂВ” Sibalom Annex in the Municipality of Sibalom, Province of Antique is hereby separated from the Pis-anan National High School and converted into an independent national high school to be known as Sibalom National High School.

SEC. 2.

Transfer of Assets and Liabilities.

ГўВЂВ” All personnel, assets, liabilities and records of the Pis-anan National High School ГўВЂВ” Sibalom Annex are hereby transferred to and absorbed by the Sibalom National High School.

SEC. 3.

Appropriations.

ГўВЂВ” The Secretary of Education shall immediately include in the Department's program the operationalization of the Sibalom National High School, the initial funding of which shall be charged against the current year's appropriations of the Pis-anan National High School ГўВЂВ” Sibalom Annex. Thereafter, the amount necessary for the continued operation of the school shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC.

Implementation.

ГўВЂВ” The Secretary of Education shall issue rules and regulations that may be necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC.

Implementation.

ГўВЂВ” The Secretary of Education shall issue rules and regulations that may be necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC. 5.

Effectivity.

ГўВЂВ” This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days afters its publication in the

Official Gazette

.

Approved,

(Sgd.) JUAN PONCE ENRILE

(Sgd.) PROSPERO C. NOGRALES

President of Senate

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on November 17, 2008 and October 13, 2009, respectively.

(Sgd.) EMMA LIRIO-REYES

(Sgd.) MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary of Senate

Secretary General House of Representatives

Approved: JAN 15 2010

(Sgd.) GLORIA MACAPAGAL - ARROYO

President of the Philippines
